aggressive.name: -Pn
aggressive.description: Treat all hosts as alive (skip discovery)

safe.name: -sS
safe.description: TCP SYN (Stealth) scan - fastest and most popular

safe.name: -sT
safe.description: TCP connect scan - used for unprivileged users or IPv6

aggressive.name: -sU
aggressive.description: UDP scan - for discovering UDP services

aggressive.name: -sA
aggressive.description: TCP ACK scan - used to map out firewall rulesets

aggressive.name: -sW
aggressive.description: TCP Window scan - detects open/closed ports via window field

aggressive.name: -sM
aggressive.description: TCP Maimon scan - obscure firewall-evading scan (FIN/ACK)

aggressive.name: -sO
aggressive.description: IP Protocol scan - determines supported protocols (TCP, ICMP, etc.)

stealth_evasion.name: -sN
stealth_evasion.description: TCP NULL scan - no bits set, sneaks past firewalls

stealth_evasion.name: -sF
stealth_evasion.description: TCP FIN scan - sets only the FIN bit

stealth_evasion.name: -sX
stealth_evasion.description: TCP Xmas scan - sets FIN, PSH, and URG flags (lights up like a tree)

stealth_evasion.name: -sI
stealth_evasion.description: Idle scan - extremely stealthy, uses a zombie host

stealth_evasion.name: -b
stealth_evasion.description: FTP Bounce scan - uses FTP proxy to scan (deprecated but useful)
